claim: Explorer and cross-checker evidence sufficiently verifies that YOLO demand evidence system implements scope distinction (project | external | user | unknown) in schema, protocols, readiness contract, and tests.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION and VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES establish the four scope categories and project evidence requirements.
evidence.why: Schema definition is the foundation that mandates evidence scope declaration and validates project facts require project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: Test suite validates external-only evidence cannot satisfy project facts, project evidence requires paths, URLs cannot be relabeled, and scope field is mandatory.
evidence.why: Tests provide evidence that acceptance criteria are met and validated through automated checks.

evidence.summary: Boundary enforcement tests validate project file mutations are blocked and detected via git state.
evidence.why: Tests verify the scope_in constraint that evidence gathering does not modify target project files.

evidence.summary: Product documentation defines evidence agent protocols, scope distinction, and project evidence requirements.
evidence.why: Documentation provides authoritative specification of scope behavior and requirements.

assumptions: Stress probes against real Claude -p agents have not been independently verified; this remains an assumption from the original explorer result.
risks: If evidenceRecordScope function has edge cases that misclassify evidence patterns, validation could be bypassed.
If boundary enforcement has false positives, legitimate evidence gathering operations could be blocked.

result.notes: Explorer and cross-checker results agree with high confidence. Evidence schema, protocols, readiness contract, and tests all implement the four scope categories (project, external, user, unknown). Acceptance criteria is satisfied: scope distinction is implemented in all required components. All evidence records include scope declarations. Project evidence requires repo-relative paths. External evidence cannot prove project facts. One assumption remains (stress probes not verified), but it does not block the core claim about scope implementation. No blockers prevent proceeding.
